name: @chbrain/khai-engine-locus-of-control
description: khai engine content, the locus-of-control domain: the generalized expectancy about where control lies as position, held before a persona reads any single outcome.
license: SEE LICENSE IN LICENSE and LICENSE-CODE

khai.tagline: Locus of control as position: the generalized expectancy about where control lies, held before a persona reads any single outcome.

card.wire: Attaches at Position. The anchor (position_locus_of_control.md) declares locus of control as a resting expectancy a persona carries into every outcome, not a read of any one event, and the system-wide law makes every persona carry it.
card.issue: Two ready expressions a persona may hold: internal and external. Each is a full position file the build composes onto the anchor, so the persona gets a standing expectancy, not a label. More expressions slot in as sibling files without moving the anchor.
card.require: Two altitudes must hold. One, the law: declared once in the project's Instructions Knowledge chapter ('personas hold a locus of control'), so the expectancy holds system-wide and no persona can drop it. Two, the link: each persona carries its expectancy under Projection. A persona may also hold a different, even contradicting, expectancy under Shadow; the gap between shown and hidden is the character, not a contradiction to reject.
card.enforce: Structure is checked; coherence is judged. The kit verifies the floor: the law is declared, and each persona carries a locus-of-control expectancy under Projection. Whether a Projection and a Shadow expectancy cohere into a true persona is semantic, so it is taught and reviewed, never failed by the suite. The engine ships its own structural tests; the reading is guided, not gated.
card.setup: Two steps. Declare the law in the Instructions Knowledge chapter, linking the anchor. Then give each persona its expectancy under Projection: one expression, which carries the anchor upward. Where a persona conceals a different expectancy, put it under Shadow. Read it in the first beat, hold it under every process; the room enacts it, never narrates it.
